title: The Hidden Leadership Skills Every Woman Already Possesses
excerpt: In her first solo episode, Aimee Rickabus reveals how the invisible work women do daily is actually powerful leadership in disguise. She shares raw stories from her journey as a mother of six and CEO, offering a new perspective on what it truly means to lead.
metaDescription: Discover why the invisible work you do every day is actually leadership. CEO and mom of six Aimee Rickabus shares her breakthrough insights.
keyTakeaways: The invisible work women perform daily—organizing, coordinating, emotional labor—is actually sophisticated leadership in action.
Leadership skills don't only develop in corporate boardrooms; they're honed through the complex demands of managing family life.
Recognizing your existing leadership abilities is the first step to claiming your power in all areas of life.
The juggling act of motherhood, marriage, and career teaches invaluable lessons about prioritization and decision-making.
Women need to start giving themselves credit for the incredible management skills they demonstrate every single day.
The journey from breakdown to breakthrough often begins with acknowledging the leadership you're already demonstrating.
Self-growth and family life aren't competing priorities—they can fuel and strengthen each other when properly balanced.

pullQuotes.text: The invisible work we do is leadership, and it's time we start treating it like it is.

timestamps.label: Life as a Mom of Six
The Story Behind 'The Manage Her' Book

content:
There's a moment in every woman's life when she realizes she's been leading all along—she just never called it that. For Aimee Rickabus, CEO of a nine-figure technology company and mother of six, this revelation became the foundation of both her book and her mission to help women recognize the powerful leadership skills they already possess.

In this deeply personal solo episode of The Manage Her Podcast, Aimee pulls back the curtain on what it really means to navigate the beautiful chaos of modern womanhood. With children ranging from toddlers to grown adults, she's living proof that leadership isn't confined to corner offices or boardroom presentations—it's happening in your kitchen, your car, and every conversation where you're coordinating, problem-solving, and making things work.

The Leadership You Don't See

Here's what nobody tells you about leadership: you're probably already doing it, but you're calling it something else. When you're managing schedules for multiple family members, negotiating bedtimes with a toddler, or finding creative solutions to unexpected problems, you're not just handling things—you're leading. Aimee's journey to this realization wasn't immediate or easy, but it fundamentally changed how she viewed her own capabilities.

The invisible work that women do daily requires every skill taught in expensive leadership programs. You're constantly assessing situations, making strategic decisions, managing resources, and motivating team members (also known as your family). You're problem-solving on the fly, adapting to changing circumstances, and maintaining morale even when you're running on empty yourself. These aren't just survival skills—they're sophisticated leadership competencies that deserve recognition and respect.

What makes this recognition so powerful is that it shifts your entire perspective on your own abilities. Instead of feeling like you're just managing household chaos or just keeping everyone fed and on schedule, you begin to see the strategic thinking, emotional intelligence, and executive function skills you're demonstrating every single day. This shift in perspective doesn't just boost confidence—it opens up possibilities you might never have considered before.

From Overwhelm to Ownership

Aimee's story resonates because it's built on the foundation of real experience, not theoretical frameworks. Managing six children while running a nine-figure company isn't a balancing act—it's a masterclass in priority management, delegation, and strategic thinking. The skills required to keep a large family functioning smoothly are the same skills that drive successful businesses: clear communication, efficient systems, adaptive problem-solving, and the ability to see the big picture while managing immediate needs.

The breakthrough often comes when you stop apologizing for having a full life and start owning the incredible complexity you navigate daily. Every woman who's ever felt stretched thin needs to understand that being able to handle multiple competing priorities isn't a sign that you're doing too much—it's evidence that you're capable of extraordinary leadership. The key is learning to see it that way yourself.

This perspective shift transforms everything. When you recognize that coordinating family schedules requires the same skills as project management, or that mediating sibling conflicts uses the same emotional intelligence as resolving workplace disputes, you begin to understand the transferable value of your experience. You're not starting from zero when you decide to pursue new opportunities—you're building on a foundation of proven leadership experience.

The Heart Behind The Message

What makes Aimee's message so compelling is its foundation in lived experience rather than abstract theory. The Manage Her book emerged from her recognition that countless women are demonstrating remarkable leadership abilities while simultaneously feeling unseen and undervalued. The disconnect between what women are actually accomplishing and how they're perceiving their own capabilities represents a massive untapped potential.

This isn't about adding more to your already full plate or pushing yourself to achieve more external markers of success. It's about recognizing the leadership you're already demonstrating and allowing that recognition to inform how you see yourself and your possibilities. When you understand that you're already leading, you can begin to lead more intentionally in all areas of your life.

The beautiful chaos that Aimee describes isn't something to overcome or organize away—it's the training ground where real leadership skills are developed. The ability to remain calm under pressure, think creatively when resources are limited, and maintain focus on what truly matters while managing constant interruptions are exactly the qualities that distinguish effective leaders in any context.

Permission to See Yourself Clearly

Perhaps the most powerful message in this episode is the permission it gives women to see themselves clearly and completely. You don't have to wait for external validation or formal recognition to acknowledge your own leadership abilities. You don't need a title, a promotion, or someone else's permission to recognize the sophisticated management skills you demonstrate every day.

The journey from breakdown to breakthrough begins with this recognition, but it doesn't end there. Once you understand that you're already leading, the question becomes: how do you want to lead? What kind of impact do you want to have? How can you use these skills more intentionally to create the life and career you actually want?

Aimee's story is ultimately an invitation to join a different conversation—one where women's daily leadership is recognized, valued, and leveraged for greater impact. It's a reminder that you're already equipped with incredible capabilities, and it's time to start treating yourself like the leader you've always been.

quiz.title: What's Your Hidden Leadership Style?
quiz.description: You've been leading all along—you just might not have called it that. Discover the unique leadership strengths you're already using every day.

questions.question: When your family is facing a hectic week with overlapping activities and deadlines, your instinct is to:

options.text: Create a detailed schedule and coordinate everyone's needs in advance
Check in with each person individually to see how they're feeling about everything
Figure out creative solutions as things come up throughout the week
Stay calm and provide steady support so everyone feels grounded

questions.question: At work or in volunteer situations, you find yourself naturally:

options.text: Taking on the role of organizing projects and keeping track of all the details
Being the person others come to when they need someone to listen or give advice
Jumping in wherever help is needed and adapting to whatever the situation requires
Creating a supportive environment where everyone feels comfortable contributing

questions.question: When someone asks for your help with a problem, you typically:

options.text: Help them break it down into manageable steps and create a plan
Listen carefully to understand what they're really struggling with emotionally
Brainstorm creative solutions and help them see new possibilities
Offer consistent encouragement and remind them of their strengths

questions.question: Your friends and family would say your greatest strength in stressful situations is:

options.text: Your ability to see the whole picture and keep everyone organized
Your skill at reading the room and knowing exactly what people need
Your talent for thinking on your feet and finding unexpected solutions
Your calming presence that helps everyone feel more secure

questions.question: When you think about your daily routine, you feel most energized by:

options.text: Successfully coordinating multiple moving parts and seeing everything flow smoothly
The meaningful conversations and connections you have with people throughout the day
The variety and unpredictability that keeps you problem-solving and adapting
Being the reliable constant that others can count on no matter what happens

questions.question: If you were to describe your approach to handling responsibilities, you'd say:

options.text: I like to plan ahead and make sure all the pieces fit together perfectly
I focus on understanding what really matters to the people involved
I stay flexible and ready to pivot when circumstances change
I provide consistent, dependable follow-through that others can rely on

results.title: The Strategic Orchestrator
results.description: You see the big picture and naturally coordinate moving pieces to create harmony. Your leadership style is about bringing order to chaos and helping everyone succeed together. You excel at long-term planning and making sure nothing falls through the cracks. This episode will help you recognize how your natural organizing abilities are actually sophisticated leadership skills that deserve recognition and can be leveraged in every area of your life.

results.title: The Intuitive Connector
results.description: Your leadership superpower lies in reading people and situations with remarkable accuracy. You lead through understanding, empathy, and creating genuine connections that motivate others. You naturally sense what people need before they ask and excel at building trust. This episode will validate how your emotional intelligence and people skills are powerful leadership tools that can transform both your personal and professional relationships.

results.title: The Adaptive Problem-Solver
results.description: You thrive in the moment, turning challenges into opportunities with creative thinking and quick action. Your leadership style is flexible, resourceful, and gets things done even when the path isn't clear. You're the person others turn to when they need solutions fast. This episode will show you how your ability to pivot and find creative solutions under pressure are executive-level leadership skills that make you invaluable in any situation.

results.title: The Steady Foundation
results.description: Your leadership strength is creating stability and consistency that others can count on. You lead through reliability, patience, and creating safe spaces where others can grow and take risks. Your calm presence and steady guidance provide the foundation that allows teams and families to thrive. This episode will help you see how your nurturing, supportive approach is a powerful form of leadership that creates lasting positive change in people's lives.
